title: Compliance Risk Assessment: Clearview AI — Facial Recognition Platform
use_case_summary:
**AI System:** Clearview AI — Facial Recognition Platform
**Description:** An AI-powered facial recognition system that scrapes billions of images from public web sources (social media, news sites, mugshot databases) to build a biometric database. It matches submitted images against the database to identify individuals and is sold primarily to law enforcement, intelligence agencies, and private clients.
**Domain:** Law Enforcement
**Personal Data:** Yes
**Sensitive Data:** Yes
**Automated Decisions:** Yes
**Impacts Rights:** Yes
explanation: The Clearview AI facial recognition platform is classified as High Risk due to its use of biometric data for identification purposes, which raises significant privacy and fairness concerns. Article 9 mandates the establishment of a documented risk management system, while Article 10 requires data governance policies, and Article 14 emphasizes the need for human oversight measures, all of which are critical given the potential adverse effects on individuals' rights and freedoms. Compliance concerns identified: Privacy Concern, Fairness / Bias Concern, Human Oversight Concern. Classification confidence: 85%

recommended_mitigations: Establish and document a comprehensive Risk Management System (RMS) covering the entire lifecycle of the AI system as per Article 9.
Develop and implement a Data Governance Policy and conduct a Quality Assessment Report to comply with Article 10.
Prepare comprehensive Technical Documentation in accordance with Annex IV before placing the system on the market as required by Article 11.
Create and implement Human-in-the-loop operational guidelines to ensure human oversight in decision-making processes as stipulated in Article 14.
Conduct regular audits and assessments to ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations regarding the processing of biometric data.

obligations.description: Establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system.
obligations.evidence_needed: Documented Risk Management System (RMS) covering the entire lifecycle.
Data Governance Policy and Quality Assessment Report.

obligations.description: Technical documentation must be drawn up before the system is placed on the market.
obligations.evidence_needed: Comprehensive Technical Documentation as per Annex IV.
Human-in-the-loop operational guidelines and UI/UX evidence.
